schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: Income Tax Valuation — Rule 11UA FMV
schemas.description: Income Tax valuation by IBBI Registered Valuer. Rule 11UA/11UAA fair market value. Section 56(2)(x) anti-abuse compliance. Share issuance, transfer, and buyback valuations.

mainEntity.name: Is Rule 11UA valuation still needed after angel tax abolition?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. While Section 56(2)(viib) (angel tax) was abolished in July 2024, Section 56(2)(x) continues to apply. Any share issuance at premium still requires Rule 11UA FMV determination to avoid tax liability on the excess.

mainEntity.name: What methods are accepted under Rule 11UA?

acceptedAnswer.text: For unquoted equity shares: NAV method or DCF method (the higher value applies). DCF must be certified by a SEBI-registered Merchant Banker or a Chartered Accountant. For preference shares: present value of expected dividends plus redemption.

mainEntity.name: Can Rule 11UA and FEMA valuations give different values for the same shares?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, frequently. Rule 11UA requires the higher of NAV and DCF. FEMA requires DCF only. The Rule 11UA value may be higher (if NAV exceeds DCF) or different due to different discount rate assumptions. We prepare dual-framework reports.

mainEntity.name: What if the AO challenges the Rule 11UA valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Assessing Officer can refer the valuation to a Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) under Section 142A. Having a robust valuation report with sensitivity analysis, multiple scenario testing, and clear assumption documentation significantly strengthens your position in assessment.

mainEntity.name: Does Rule 11UAA change anything for domestic share issuance?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rule 11UAA (effective AY 2024-25) primarily extended valuation requirements to consideration received from any person, harmonising domestic and cross-border treatment. The methodologies remain the same as Rule 11UA.
